lesson.title: Welcome to PY101!
masteryOutcomes: Feel welcomed into PY101 and motivated to begin learning Python.
Understand the PY101 structure: Writing your first program, Core foundations, Decisions and loops, and the Introduction to Python summary.
Understand that each unit ends with a review: reviews 1-3 are 10% each, untimed, with unlimited retakes.
Understand the final unit review is 60%, timed, with one retake; weekly discussions are 10%; 10 bonus points for passing all reviews on time.

concept: Orientation: how PY101 works and why you are here

explanations.text: Welcome! PY101 teaches you to write real Python, starting from zero. You will learn a little at a time and build up to writing your own programs.
PY101 has four units: Writing your first program, Core foundations, Decisions and loops, and an Introduction to Python summary. You move through short lessons, then each unit ends with a review.

Grading: reviews 1-3 are worth 10% each, are untimed, and allow unlimited retakes. The final unit review is worth 60%, is timed, and allows one retake. Weekly discussions add 10%, and passing all reviews on time earns 10 bonus points.

misconceptions.trigger: Thinks they need prior coding experience to start
misconceptions.correction: PY101 assumes no background. Reassure the student and start from the very first step.

checks.prompt: Before we start: what do you want to get out of learning Python, and are you ready to write your first line of code?
acceptanceCriteria: Student shares a goal or motivation, or confirms they are ready to begin
checks.passFeedback: Love it — let's turn that into your first running program.
checks.failFeedback: No worries — even "I just want to try it" is a great reason. Ready when you are.

pedagogyRules: Open warmly and build excitement; this is the first impression.
If the student shares a name or preference, honor it for the rest of the session.
If the student sounds nervous or unsure, acknowledge the feeling before explaining anything.
Set expectations clearly and simply; never overwhelm with the full grading rules unless asked.

failurePlaybooks.correctMove: If the student says to call them a certain name, use it from then on and never revert.
When the student expresses nerves or doubt, acknowledge it first and reassure before moving into content.

chunks.text: PY101 is the first course in the AI Engineering program. It teaches coding from scratch with a focus on Python, for learners with no prior technical background.
PY101 is organized into four units: Writing your first program, Core foundations, Decisions and loops, and an Introduction to Python summary.

Each unit ends with a review. Reviews 1-3 are 10% each, untimed, with unlimited retakes. The final review is 60%, timed, one retake. Weekly discussions are 10%, and 10 bonus points are available for passing all reviews on time.

celebration.headline: You are set up and ready to learn.
celebration.recap: You know how PY101 is structured and what to expect.
celebration.unlocked: Next: write and run your very first line of Python.
